document.purpose: Rules and patterns cheat sheet for AI/agent preflight. Read first, then task-specific docs.

doc_guide.1_ARCHITECTURE.md: System design, data model, storage, invariants, delete/version policy, AI control
doc_guide.2.2_WORKFLOWS_CHAT.md: Chat retrieval generation, cache layers, Soi sáng
doc_guide.3_API_CONTRACTS.md: Full route reference, security, rate limits, error handling
doc_guide.4_DEPLOYMENT.md: Docker topology, Traefik v3.7.1, env vars, health probes, backup
doc_guide.5_NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md: Variable, function, class, file naming standards

v0.1_performance_features.semantic_cache: Similarity match > 98% (dist < 0.08) via Redis Vector Search (Async), 24h TTL
v0.1_performance_features.binary_history: MessagePack serialization for sub-millisecond chat hydration (Redis RPUSH + pipeline atomic)
v0.1_performance_features.decoupled_audit: XADD stream logging (<1ms) with background AuditStreamWorker
v0.1_performance_features.bloom_duplicate: BF.EXISTS check for O(1) document upload skipping (Redis 8.x)
v0.1_performance_features.cpu_offloading: Strict asyncio.to_thread usage for OCR, Tokenization, and Embedding to prevent loop blocking
v0.1_performance_features.bm25_singleton: TTL-cached vocabulary in RAM (120s) to handle high concurrency without Redis bottlenecks

rechunk_rule: POST /documents/{id}/rechunk auto-detects: if ocr_output.md exists in S3 → rechunk from OCR (free); if not → fallback to retry (re-upload to LlamaParse). Frontend shows single RefreshCw button for both ready/failed statuses — no separate retry button needed.

core_invariants.name: async_ingestion
core_invariants.rule: Upload returns task_id immediately. Parsing/indexing runs asynchronously via Celery workers.

core_invariants.name: five_stage_retrieval
core_invariants.rule: Hybrid search (dense + BM25 RRF fusion) → section grouping (≥0.30) → Rerank (AI-Engine, top 5) → Neighbor Expansion (Soi sáng) → full section context assembly + citations. 3-layer cache: LLM Response (4h) → Semantic (24h) → Query Embedding (4h). Retrieve at chunk level, present expanded section context to LLM. Doc IDs TTL-cached 60s.

core_invariants.name: hard_delete_order
core_invariants.rule: 6 steps: registry → vectors → sections → file → DB row → purge. Sections before DB row (referential integrity).

core_invariants.name: api_gateway_proxy
core_invariants.rule: Browser never sees Bearer token. All calls via /api/bep/ → Next.js Route Handler → getToken() → backend.

core_invariants.name: id_first_boundaries
core_invariants.rule: Cross-layer and worker payloads pass stable IDs/URIs only. Rehydrate rich state inside the owning layer.

core_invariants.name: provider_boundary
core_invariants.rule: Routes never call provider factories, adapters, or SDKs. ChatService owns AI orchestration; routes own the HTTP response shell.

core_invariants.name: v0.1_speed_layer
core_invariants.rule: Semantic Cache (Redis) → MessagePack History → Hybrid Search → Audit Stream → BM25 Singleton. All high-frequency IO is non-blocking or cached.

data_location_map.notes: Canonical order source (order_index). Accessed via AsyncSession.
~400 token chunks with section_id metadata. Named dense + sparse-bm25 vectors. AsyncQdrantClient.
Singleton BM25Manager with 120s TTL. Vocabulary loaded from Redis and cached in-memory.
DB persisted; Redis hot cache uses MessagePack binary serialization for speed/RAM.
Hard-deleted after 30 days by Celery Beat. Sidebar ordered by updated_at DESC.
Redis Vector Search (RediSearch) for rapid intent matching.
Redis cache 5min TTL; injected into AI systemInstruction
Token revocation on logout. Role cached in JWT payload.

ai.AI_PROXY_DEFAULT_MODEL: model name in 9Router prefix/model format (can be empty)

anti_patterns: direct provider SDK calls in routes
direct raise HTTPException in API layer (use http_errors helpers)
SessionLocal or SQLAlchemy Session in route files (use Repository via Service)
business logic in route handlers (belongs in Service layer)

architecture_layers.description: Strict 3-layer separation. Routes = HTTP only. Services = business logic. Repositories = data access. All layers strictly await I/O.

controller.responsibility: HTTP parsing, auth deps, response formatting, status codes. NO business logic, NO AsyncSessionLocal inside routes (use deps).

canonical_dict_keys.artifact_metadata: Use 'artifact_metadata' in all dictionaries, schemas, and pipeline outputs representing arbitrary extra JSON metadata (replaces legacy 'extra_metadata', 'metadata').

files.chat/services/service.py: ChatService — prepare_chat, CLIProxyBridge stream orchestration, session mgmt, message persistence, memory extraction dispatch
files.chat/retrieval/retrieval_service.py: RetrievalService — 5-stage retrieval (hybrid → group → rerank → expand → context)
files.chat/retrieval/expansion_service.py: ExpansionService — multi-query domain logic for RAG
files.documents/ingestion/ingestion_service.py: IngestionService — accepts optional section_repo via constructor

repository.convention: repository.py or repositories/repository.py naming. Takes Session via constructor, returns dicts (not ORM models)

di_wiring.pattern: Repository factories yield from SessionLocal, Service factories take Repository via Depends()

priority: Preserve public APIs and route contracts
Prefer existing service layers and adapters
do: Read AGENTS.md first, then 00_QUICK_REFERENCE, then task-specific docs
Reuse existing types, constants, and helpers
Use nh3 for HTML sanitization, PyJWT for JWT
do_not: Invent new architecture unless docs require it
Rewrite unrelated files during small fixes
Change endpoint names or response fields silently
Return confident answers without grounded evidence

infrastructure.hardware_auto_detect: app/core/hardware.py — PROD tier: 4-8 workers, 100+ DB/Redis pool for 200+ CCU. TIGHT tier: 1 worker for low VRAM (<6GB).
infrastructure.async_policy: All I/O is non-blocking. Heavy CPU (OCR, Embed, Rerank) is offloaded to threads via asyncio.to_thread.
infrastructure.redis_8.x_config: maxmemory via REDIS_MAXMEMORY env var (default 512mb for dev), allkeys-lru, save 60 1000, appendonly yes
infrastructure.redis_db_separation: DB 0: App cache + RediSearch (semantic cache requires db 0), DB 1: Celery result backend, DB 2: Celery broker
infrastructure.celery_config: All values configurable via env vars: time limits, memory limit, visibility timeout, retry, max-tasks-per-child. Workers service uses celery multi with 2 nodes: node-ingestion (solo, GPU, ingestion queue) + node-default (prefork, CPU, cleanup+default queues, Beat scheduler). Routing configured in ops/entrypoint-worker.sh

ingestion_queue: rechunk_document_task — re-chunk from saved OCR markdown, no LlamaParse API (upload_tasks.py)
rebuild_bm25_index_task — BM25 vocab rebuild from Qdrant after upload/delete (maintenance_tasks.py)
cleanup_queue: cleanup_old_chat_sessions_task — session TTL cleanup (cleanup_tasks.py, Beat daily)
cleanup_orphaned_vectors_task — remove Qdrant vectors without DB sections (maintenance_tasks.py, Beat daily)
default_queue: save_chat_message_task — compatibility wrapper; SSE path uses synchronous save before done:true (chat_tasks.py)
process_audit_stream — batch persist Redis stream to DB (audit_worker.py, Beat/Worker)

infrastructure.api_resource_limits: 4 CPUs, 6G RAM (Docker deploy limits) — increase for production
